---
title: "Cheatsheet · SHAP variants & equations"
subtitle: "From prediction to intervention"
---

::: {.cheatsheet}

## The Shapley value

For a value function $v$ over feature coalitions, feature $i$'s attribution is
the average marginal contribution over all orderings:
$$ \phi_i = \sum_{S \subseteq N \setminus \{i\}} \frac{|S|!\,(|N|-|S|-1)!}{|N|!}\,\big[v(S \cup \{i\}) - v(S)\big]. $$
The variants differ only in **how $v(S)$ is defined**.

## Four value functions

| Variant | $v(S)$ uses | Answers |
|---|---|---|
| **Conditional** | $\mathbb{E}[f(X)\mid X_S = x_S]$ | what the model predicts given observed S |
| **Interventional** | $\mathbb{E}[f(X_S = x_S, X_{\bar S})]$ | drop features by marginalizing (Janzing 2020) |
| **Asymmetric** | interventional, coalitions **respecting a causal order** | credit distal causes first (Frye 2020) |
| **Structural / causal** | $\mathbb{E}[f(X)\mid do(X_S = x_S)]$ | what changes downstream under intervention (Heskes 2020) |

## The structural recipe (this project)

1. **Abduct** exogenous noise from a background row.
2. **Intervene:** apply $do(X_S = x_S)$.
3. **Propagate** through descendants in the SCM.
4. **Score** the model; average marginal contributions over DAG-consistent orders.

The Shapley **efficiency** axiom holds exactly: attributions sum to
$f(x) - \mathbb{E}[f]$ (verified to ~1e-15 in the build).

## Diagnosing the homunculus

[PBI — Proximity Bias Index.]{.lead-in} How much attribution mass sits near the
outcome. High PBI = homunculus. Structural Causal SHAP drives PBI toward zero.

::: {.pullquote}
Ordinary SHAP is not "wrong" — it faithfully answers a prediction question.
Structural Causal SHAP answers an intervention question. Pick the value function
that matches your decision.
:::

## Python, today

Interventional SHAP is native (`shap`, `feature_perturbation="interventional"`).
True causal/asymmetric Shapley lives in R `shapr`; the structural engine here is
implemented directly in `causal_shap.structural_value`.

:::

*Print for a handout.* See [rung 3](../workflow/03-causal-shap.qmd).
